description.from:
## Summary
Add a first-class misclassification-classification layer to evaluation analysis so incorrect items are classified by what kind of problem they represent, rather than assuming every error is fixable by score optimization.

## Scope
- Each misclassified item is classified into exactly one of four operator-facing categories: , , , or .
- Evaluations also gain evaluation-level red flags for cross-item mechanical/pathological patterns that are not item-local, such as all predictions collapsing to one class or score results containing execution errors.
- The dashboard evaluation view gains a visual breakdown of the misclassification categories plus item-level drill-down.
- This work remains distinct from deterministic reference datasets and distinct from RCA reliability/contract work.

## Key assumptions
- Feedback contradictions remain a separate report/workstream and are not folded into the per-item four-category classifier in v1.
- The new classification layer becomes part of the broader RCA pipeline, not an unrelated side feature.
- Public-facing web app documentation is required as part of this feature.
description.to:
## Summary
Add a first-class misclassification-classification layer to evaluation analysis so incorrect items are classified by what kind of problem they represent, rather than assuming every error is fixable by score optimization.

## Scope
- Each misclassified item is classified into exactly one of four operator-facing categories: `score_configuration_problem`, `information_gap`, `guideline_gap_requires_sme`, or `mechanical_malfunction`.
- Evaluations also gain evaluation-level red flags for cross-item mechanical/pathological patterns that are not item-local, such as all predictions collapsing to one class or score results containing execution errors.
- The dashboard evaluation view gains a visual breakdown of the misclassification categories plus item-level drill-down.
- This work remains distinct from deterministic reference datasets and distinct from RCA reliability/contract work.

## Key assumptions
- Feedback contradictions remain a separate report/workstream and are not folded into the per-item four-category classifier in v1.
- The new classification layer becomes part of the broader RCA pipeline, not an unrelated side feature.
- Public-facing web app documentation is required as part of this feature.
